title: Define kernel runtime value model for declared column types
description:
Context:
The mutation kernel needs one explicit runtime value model before coercion, storage, or constraint enforcement can be implemented safely.

Deliverables:
- runtime value representation for the declared column-type subset
- null/value semantics aligned with the declared v0 surface
- focused invariants tests for the value model
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared column types in the v0 subset have one explicit runtime value representation.
2. Null/value semantics are defined in one place instead of reappearing ad hoc in storage and constraints.
3. The value model is reusable by mutation and later read execution.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/value.rs and value-model tests.
Reservation guidance: own the value representation only.

title: Wire the unchanged extractor canary against the live twin harness
description:
Context:
Once the representative extractor and fixture set are frozen, the live canary wiring can stay focused on execution and assertions.

Deliverables:
- unchanged extractor execution against the live twin
- focused assertions for the subset the extractor actually uses
- clear failure output when runtime behavior drifts from the manifested subset
acceptance_criteria:
1. The pinned extractor runs unchanged against the live twin for the declared subset.
2. Drift from the manifested subset causes a focused canary failure.
3. Harness wiring stays separate from fixture/corpus selection.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/extractor_canary.rs and live harness support.
Reservation guidance: own live harness wiring only.

title: Land core Operation IR and kernel result types for twinning
description:
Context:
The plan now makes both the Operation IR and kernel result contract explicit, but the repo still lacks a concrete module for them. This issue lands that shared type surface first.

Deliverables:
- explicit IR types for SessionOp, PrepareOp, MutationOp, ReadOp, and RefusalOp
- explicit kernel result types for AckResult, ReadResult, MutationResult, and RefusalResult
- controlled vocab definitions aligned to the plan
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo has one dedicated IR/result surface that protocol and kernel code can both consume.
2. The types encode the controlled vocab named in the plan.
3. The IR/result layer contains no protocol frames or backend-specific residency hints.
notes:
Target files: future src/ir.rs or equivalent shared module surface.
Reservation guidance: own the shared contract only; do not let protocol or backend details leak in here.

title: Implement declared read/query subset and phase-3 corpus parity for twinning
description:
Context:
The plan has an explicit phase-3 read/query milestone, but the current Beads graph had been mixing core read execution with ORM metadata stubs. This issue is now the read-kernel lane only: the declared SELECT subset plus update/delete for the shapes we actually claim.

Deliverables:
- point lookups, filtered scans, and declared predicate semantics
- aggregate count and basic group-by only where the manifest requires them
- update/delete by predicate for the declared subset
acceptance_criteria:
1. The declared query corpus meets the phase-3 acceptance budget in the plan.
2. Unsupported query shapes are explicit protocol refusals or replay-time SKIPs, never silent partial behavior.
3. Query execution stays behind the shared kernel/result surface instead of protocol-local shortcuts.
4. ORM/catalog metadata stubs are not required to close this lane.
notes:
Target files: future src/kernel/read_scan.rs, src/kernel/predicate.rs, src/kernel/aggregate.rs, and src/kernel/update_delete.rs plus query-focused differential fixtures.
Reservation guidance: this is the explicit phase-3 kernel lane; ORM metadata lives in bd-1ot.

title: Implement Describe/Execute/Sync dispatch for the declared extended-query subset
description:
Context:
Extended-query execution should stay separate from Parse/Bind state so parameterized read and mutation dispatch can be tested without reworking statement-cache mechanics.

Deliverables:
- Describe, Execute, and Sync handling for the declared portal subset
- dispatch from prepared state into normalized mutation and read operations
- explicit refusal coverage for unsupported Describe/Execute shapes
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared parameterized canaries complete Describe/Execute/Sync over prepared state.
2. Unsupported Describe/Execute traffic is surfaced as protocol-visible refusal.
3. Execute/Sync behavior is covered by focused tests independent of Parse/Bind state creation.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/extended_execute.rs and execute-sync tests.
Reservation guidance: own Describe/Execute/Sync dispatch only; Parse/Bind stays in a sibling bead.

title: Add startup, restore, and reset budget harness with baseline capture
description:
Context:
The total operator runtime feels too long, but the repo currently has no durable budget harness to prove changes help instead of hurt.

Deliverables:
- reusable timing harness for cold bootstrap, warm restore, and later reset paths
- baseline capture for the current Phase-0 path
- budget assertions wired to the plan's startup/restore targets once live runtime work lands
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo contains a reusable budget harness under tests/storage.
2. The current implemented path records a baseline instead of relying on anecdotal timing.
3. Later runtime/storage work can extend this harness without changing its operator-facing metric definitions.
notes:
Target files: tests/storage/budgets.rs.
Reservation guidance: own performance measurement and baseline capture only.

title: Define backend trait and base snapshot access contract
description:
Context:
Tournament-mode economics need one explicit backend contract once committed row storage exists, but before overlay behavior and snapshot semantics widen the lane.

Deliverables:
- backend trait for base snapshot access and overlay operations
- focused tests for the base backend contract over committed row storage
- boundaries that keep SQL/protocol details out of the backend surface
acceptance_criteria:
1. One explicit backend trait exists for later base/overlay implementations.
2. Backend abstractions do not leak protocol or SQL parsing details.
3. The base contract is covered by focused tests before overlay semantics land, and it does not wait on full constraint/DML completion.
notes:
Target files: src/backend/base.rs and backend contract tests.
Reservation guidance: own the backend trait/base contract only.

title: Implement INSERT and INSERT RETURNING over the write kernel
description:
Context:
INSERT semantics are the first full mutation path and should land before ON CONFLICT behavior expands the surface.

Deliverables:
- INSERT execution for the declared mutation subset
- INSERT RETURNING support for declared returning shapes
- focused mutation tests that compare observable tags, rows-affected, and returning rows
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared INSERT and INSERT RETURNING cases pass against the shared write corpus.
2. Mutation behavior stays behind the shared kernel/result surface.
3. INSERT correctness is proven before ON CONFLICT semantics widen the path.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/insert.rs and insert-focused mutation tests.
Reservation guidance: own insert semantics only.

title: Normalize session, mutation, and read subset shapes into IR with refusal coverage
description:
Context:
After the core IR types exist, the repo needs one normalization layer that turns supported SQL/session shapes into IR and unsupported shapes into explicit refusal ops.

Deliverables:
- session-control normalization for begin/commit/rollback/set/sync
- mutation normalization for the declared write subset
- read normalization for the declared read subset
- explicit refusal coverage for unsupported shapes before backend execution
acceptance_criteria:
1. Supported equivalent client/query shapes normalize to the same IR form.
2. Unsupported syntax or unsupported subset shapes become explicit RefusalOp values before backend execution.
3. Normalization keeps catalog resolution and controlled-vocab invariants intact.
notes:
Target files: future parser/normalization layer and focused normalization tests.
Reservation guidance: this issue owns normalization semantics, not protocol framing or storage.

title: Implement table storage and primary/unique lookup surfaces
description:
Context:
The write kernel needs base table storage and keyed lookup surfaces before constraint checks and upsert semantics can stay bounded and fast.

Deliverables:
- table storage for committed row state
- primary-key and unique-key lookup surfaces
- focused tests for storage identity and lookup behavior
acceptance_criteria:
1. Table storage and keyed lookup surfaces exist independently of coercion and constraint policy.
2. Primary-key and unique-key lookups are available to later constraint and upsert slices.
3. Storage tests lock the base row-state behavior before mutation semantics are added.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/storage.rs and storage lookup tests.
Reservation guidance: own base table storage and keyed lookups only.

title: Add focused IR and result invariants tests for twinning core types
description:
Context:
Once the core operation and result types exist, the repo needs tight invariants tests that lock the contract down before parser and kernel code build on top of it. This issue owns that focused contract test lane.

Deliverables:
- focused tests for allowed controlled-vocab values and required fields
- invariants coverage that equivalent future callers can share without redefining the contract
- refusal/result test cases for the normalized surface
acceptance_criteria:
1. The core IR/result surface is covered by focused invariants tests.
2. Tests fail if the controlled vocab drifts from the plan without an intentional change.
3. The coverage stays at the shared contract level rather than depending on protocol or storage behavior.
notes:
Target files: unit tests adjacent to the shared IR/result module.
Reservation guidance: keep these tests contract-focused; normalization equivalence fixtures stay in bd-ss7.

title: Implement connection session loop and transaction-status state transitions
description:
Context:
After handshake and startup metadata exist, the adapter needs a stable connection loop and transaction-status model that later protocol slices can build on.

Deliverables:
- bounded session loop for declared client traffic
- transaction-status state transitions aligned with begin/commit/rollback flows
- connection-safe handling for protocol-visible errors
acceptance_criteria:
1. The session loop stays alive across protocol-visible runtime errors where the plan requires it.
2. Transaction-status transitions align with the declared session-control subset.
3. Later simple/extended-query slices can reuse this loop without reimplementing connection state.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/session.rs and session-loop tests.
Reservation guidance: own connection/session state only.

title: Add shared twin-vs-Postgres differential runner utilities
description:
Context:
The differential suites need one common way to boot the twin, connect to the comparison Postgres target, and execute declared corpora.

Deliverables:
- shared runner utilities for twin and Postgres execution
- common fixture loading for differential cases
- stable setup/teardown behavior for parity suites
acceptance_criteria:
1. Differential suites launch and execute through one shared runner surface.
2. Setup or teardown failures are reported consistently across read and write corpora.
3. New parity suites can reuse the runner without reimplementing process plumbing.
notes:
Target files: tests/differential/support/runner.rs.
Reservation guidance: own runner plumbing only.

title: Implement live --run child orchestration and exit-domain reporting
description:
Context:
Run mode needs separate child-orchestration and final-metadata slices so process control bugs do not blur with artifact semantics. This issue is the run-mode roll-up.

Deliverables:
- child launch/wait/termination orchestration
- final run metadata attachment for exit_code, signal, and timed_out
- 0/1/2 exit-domain reporting through the final artifact surface
acceptance_criteria:
1. Child orchestration and final run-metadata child slices are both implemented.
2. Run-mode reporting stays inside the single 0/1/2 domain contract.
3. This issue remains a roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: src/runtime.rs and run-mode tests.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Implement final run metadata attachment and exit-domain reporting
description:
Context:
Run-mode metadata and exit-domain reporting are separate from child orchestration and should be isolated so final artifact semantics remain explicit.

Deliverables:
- final run metadata for exit_code, signal, and timed_out
- 0/1/2 exit-domain handling aligned with the plan
- focused tests for final artifact metadata in run mode
acceptance_criteria:
1. Final run artifacts record exit_code or signal and timed_out status when applicable.
2. twinning still exits through the single 0/1/2 domain contract.
3. Run metadata attachment is covered independently of child launch mechanics.
notes:
Target files: src/runtime/run_result.rs and run-metadata tests.
Reservation guidance: own final run metadata only.

title: Implement Postgres wire-protocol listener for twinning
description:
Context:
The pgwire adapter now has explicit responsibilities beyond "make clients connect." It owns startup/auth/session lifecycle, protocol-visible unsupported-shape handling, and single-writer admission behavior while consuming the shared Operation IR and kernel-result surface instead of inventing local semantics.

Deliverables:
- startup/auth/session handling for the v0 canaries
- protocol mapping from parsed client traffic into the shared Operation IR
- protocol-visible error mapping for unsupported live shapes without killing the twin
- single-writer admission behavior that keeps sessions alive and surfaces a clear SQLSTATE on conflicting write attempts
acceptance_criteria:
1. psql_smoke, psycopg2_params, and sqlalchemy_core can complete the declared phase-1 session flows.
2. Unsupported live shapes return protocol-visible failures (defaulting to SQLSTATE 0A000 unless the canary contract requires narrower behavior) and do not terminate the twin.
3. A conflicting second writer receives a protocol-visible SQLSTATE 55P03 while both sessions remain alive.
4. The adapter consumes the shared Operation IR and kernel-result surface rather than bypassing them.
5. Bootstrap/configuration failures remain process-level refusals before traffic is accepted.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/** and protocol-focused canary tests.
Reservation guidance: own session/protocol behavior only; do not absorb backend storage or verify semantics into the adapter.

title: Build the declared differential write corpus against real Postgres
description:
Context:
The write parity lane now has shared differential runner/comparator support. This issue owns the declared write corpus itself plus suite wiring for those cases, not the shared harness machinery.

Deliverables:
- checked-in write fixtures and expected outcomes for insert/upsert/constraint cases
- suite wiring that feeds the shared differential support layer
- explicit refusal classification for out-of-subset write shapes
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo contains a runnable write corpus using the shared differential support layer.
2. Declared write cases compare observable results and SQLSTATEs, not just row counts.
3. Unsupported write shapes are tagged explicitly rather than silently dropped from the corpus.
notes:
Target files: tests/differential/write_corpus.rs and write fixtures.
Reservation guidance: own the write corpus and suite wiring only; shared diff support lives elsewhere.

title: Implement minimal catalog and session metadata stubs required by canaries
description:
Context:
Catalog completeness is out of scope, but the canaries may still need a small set of metadata surfaces. Those should be isolated so they do not sprawl.

Deliverables:
- only the minimal catalog/session metadata surfaces proved necessary by canaries
- focused refusal coverage for broader reflection asks
- canary-backed tests for the declared metadata subset
acceptance_criteria:
1. Only canary-proven catalog/session metadata is implemented.
2. Broader reflection/catalog completeness remains explicitly unsupported.
3. Metadata behavior is covered by focused tests instead of being implied by ORM success.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/catalog.rs and metadata tests.
Reservation guidance: own minimal metadata stubs only.

title: Bind materialized relations into embedded verify and attach raw verify.report.v1 payloads
description:
Context:
The verify bridge was still too broad for one sitting. Relation binding from committed twin state and report attachment are separate seams, so this issue is now the roll-up for those child slices only.

Deliverables:
- committed-state relation binding into embedded verify inputs
- raw verify.report.v1 attachment in the twin report surface
- twin-native metrics kept separate from verify outcomes
acceptance_criteria:
1. Relation-binding and report-attachment child slices are both implemented through explicit beads.
2. Embedded verify consumes committed twin state without inventing a second constraint language.
3. This issue remains a roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: src/runtime/verify_binding.rs and src/report/verify_attachment.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Implement startup preflight ordering and bootstrap refusal boundaries for live mode
description:
Context:
The lifecycle lane is missing the startup preflight slice that determines whether live mode accepts traffic at all. That is the clean place for bootstrap/refusal ordering.

Deliverables:
- startup preflight ordering for schema/restore, verify artifacts, report/snapshot targets, and runtime config
- process-level refusal behavior before live traffic is accepted
- focused tests for preflight ordering and refusal boundaries
acceptance_criteria:
1. Startup preflight order matches the plan before live traffic is accepted.
2. Bootstrap/configuration failures remain process-level refusals with no live traffic accepted.
3. Preflight ordering is locked by focused tests rather than implicit runtime behavior.
notes:
Target files: src/runtime/startup.rs and startup-preflight tests.
Reservation guidance: own startup preflight only.

title: Add bootstrap snapshot determinism and restore round-trip regression coverage
description:
Context:
Snapshot determinism is already part of the current contract, and later runtime work will pressure that path.

Deliverables:
- regression tests for repeated identical snapshot hashes
- restore round-trip tests for current bootstrap snapshots
- focused checks that non-semantic metadata stays outside the hash surface
acceptance_criteria:
1. Identical bootstrap state yields identical snapshot hashes across repeated runs.
2. Restore then re-emit preserves the current bootstrap snapshot contract.
3. Snapshot hash drift is caught by deterministic regression tests.
notes:
Target files: tests/bootstrap_snapshot.rs and snapshot fixtures.
Reservation guidance: own snapshot determinism and restore regressions only.
close_reason: Added bootstrap snapshot determinism and restore round-trip regression coverage

title: Normalize session control and prepared-statement flow into IR
description:
Context:
The normalization lane needs one focused slice for session lifecycle and minimal prepared-statement control before protocol work can map client traffic cleanly.

Deliverables:
- normalization for BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, tracked SET, and SYNC
- normalization for the minimal prepare/bind/execute state the canaries require
- explicit RefusalOp coverage for unsupported session/control shapes
acceptance_criteria:
1. Supported session-control shapes normalize into the declared SessionOp or PrepareOp forms.
2. Unsupported session/control shapes become explicit refusal values before backend execution.
3. The normalization stays independent of pgwire frame structs.
notes:
Target files: future normalization layer and session-focused tests.
Reservation guidance: keep this slice about session/control normalization; write and read shapes stay in sibling issues.

title: Resolve twinning v1 boundaries after drift pass
description:
Context:
The first drift pass aligned twinning with PLAN_FACTORY sequencing, removed compare-as-tool assumptions, and updated verify references to the compiled constraint protocol. A second pass is needed to settle the remaining v1 boundaries before deeper implementation work.

Decision topics:
- whether the CLI should keep --rules as the user-facing flag or rename to --constraints / --verify
- whether twinning should execute verify via an embedded library surface or via export-and-run batch invocation in v1
- what belongs in the twinning report versus verify.report / benchmark / assess outputs
- whether Twin A / Twin B migration proof remains in the v1 plan or moves to a later mode after Postgres tournament mode
- whether non-SQL adapters stay in this plan or move to a separate future document
- whether the SQL subset table should be replaced by a canary-defined manifest only
close_reason: Resolved by the March 8 boundary pass: --verify naming, embedded verify execution, attached verify report semantics, canary-defined subset, and later-mode migration proof are now reflected in the docs.

title: Implement Parse/Bind state and refusal handling for the declared extended-query subset
description:
Context:
The extended-query lane is still too broad for one sitting. Parse/Bind state and refusal handling can land independently of Execute/Sync dispatch if the state contract is explicit.

Deliverables:
- Parse and Bind handling for the declared prepared-statement subset
- statement/portal state sufficient for later Execute/Sync dispatch
- explicit refusal coverage for unsupported Parse/Bind shapes and parameter arity mismatches
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared Parse/Bind cases produce reusable prepared or portal state for the execute slice.
2. Unsupported Parse/Bind traffic is surfaced as protocol-visible refusal without killing the session.
3. Parse/Bind behavior is covered by focused tests independent of Execute/Sync row delivery.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/extended_parse.rs and parse-bind tests.
Reservation guidance: own Parse/Bind state only; Execute/Sync stays in a sibling bead.

title: Implement shared compatibility harness utilities for twinning canaries
description:
Context:
The manifest and scaffolded test layout now exist, but the real compatibility suite still needs shared harness utilities before client-specific canaries land cleanly.

Deliverables:
- shared startup/teardown helpers for launching a twin under test
- manifest-aware test utilities for canary selection and fixture lookup
- common client-facing assertions for refusal and SQLSTATE behavior
acceptance_criteria:
1. Compatibility harnesses can launch and tear down a twin through one shared utility surface.
2. Harness code reads the checked-in manifest rather than duplicating shape names ad hoc.
3. New client canaries can reuse the same utilities without redefining startup/refusal logic.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/** and shared support helpers once introduced.
Reservation guidance: own harness plumbing only; do not bury runtime semantics inside test-only helpers.

title: Add refusal and exit-domain regression coverage for current bootstrap failures
description:
Context:
Process-level refusals and exit codes are part of the current operator contract and must not drift as live paths are introduced.

Deliverables:
- tests for malformed input, malformed snapshot, and invalid bootstrap combinations
- assertions for current exit-code meanings
- refusal-envelope checks for current process-level failures
acceptance_criteria:
1. Current bootstrap failure paths are covered by explicit refusal and exit-code tests.
2. Exit-code drift causes a focused regression failure.
3. Process-level refusal output stays distinct from future client-visible runtime errors.
notes:
Target files: tests/bootstrap_refusals.rs.
Reservation guidance: own bootstrap refusal regression only.

title: Implement bounded-memory overlay and pluggable replay backend for twinning
description:
Context:
The backend lane now owns more than memory economy. It must implement the single-writer overlay model and the canonical v0 snapshot encoding so determinism and restore behavior do not drift between backends.

Deliverables:
- shared base snapshot plus per-agent bounded-memory overlay
- reader visibility over last committed state while one writer holds the mutable overlay
- deterministic reset and restore semantics
- canonical v0 snapshot encoding over full committed relation contents, not overlay deltas
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tournament twins meet the reset/startup/private-RSS budgets in the plan.
2. Readers see only last committed state; uncommitted writes remain session-local to the owning writer.
3. v0 snapshot hashes are computed from one canonical full-contents encoding, not alternate overlay-delta encodings.
4. Repeated restore/reset cycles preserve deterministic state and snapshot hashes for identical committed contents.
5. Backend work does not bypass the shared IR/kernel boundary.
notes:
Target files: backend/overlay/snapshot surfaces plus storage tests.
Reservation guidance: own storage economics and canonical snapshot behavior here; protocol framing and verify attachment stay elsewhere.

title: Add manifest-aware fixture lookup and canary selection helpers
description:
Context:
The checked-in manifest is now normative, so the harness needs one shared way to resolve canary IDs, fixture paths, and declared subset claims from it.

Deliverables:
- helper APIs to load and validate the checked-in canary manifest
- fixture lookup helpers keyed by manifest canary IDs
- canary-selection helpers that keep test names aligned with the manifest
acceptance_criteria:
1. Harness code reads the checked-in manifest through one shared helper surface.
2. Fixture lookup is keyed by declared canary IDs rather than hard-coded path conventions scattered across tests.
3. Manifest drift becomes a focused helper/test failure rather than latent per-canary breakage.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/support/** and manifest-related test helpers.
Reservation guidance: own manifest/fixture lookup only; do not absorb process launch or SQL assertions.

title: Implement pgwire startup handshake and auth acceptance shell
description:
Context:
The protocol lane first needs a bounded startup/auth shell that lets real clients complete the initial handshake without dragging in later query behavior.

Deliverables:
- startup message parsing and protocol version acceptance for the declared canaries
- bounded auth acceptance path for the supported local/dev model
- clean startup refusal behavior for malformed or unsupported startup requests
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared clients can complete the initial pgwire startup/auth handshake.
2. Malformed or unsupported startup requests fail through a protocol-visible startup refusal, not a process exit.
3. This slice stays confined to startup/auth handling and does not claim query execution.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/startup.rs and startup-focused tests.
Reservation guidance: own startup/auth handshake only.

title: Implement declared aggregate count and basic group-by over read scans
description:
Context:
Aggregate behavior should remain bounded and manifest-backed, so it needs its own explicit slice rather than leaking into generic scan execution.

Deliverables:
- count and basic group-by execution only where the manifest requires them
- focused aggregate tests over declared read shapes
- refusal coverage for broader aggregate/group-by asks
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared aggregate/group-by cases pass without implying broader SQL support.
2. Unsupported aggregate breadth is refused explicitly.
3. Aggregate logic builds on the shared scan and predicate layers instead of replacing them.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/aggregate.rs and aggregate tests.
Reservation guidance: own aggregate logic only.

title: Implement psycopg2_params canary against the mutation path
description:
Context:
psycopg2_params is the first parameterized mutation canary and the main proof that extended-query flow and write semantics meet the declared subset.

Deliverables:
- runnable psycopg2 canary covering parameterized insert/select/upsert
- a known unique-violation assertion for SQLSTATE parity
- fixture coverage for the exact mutation subset claimed in the manifest
acceptance_criteria:
1. The canary passes through the live twin with unmodified psycopg2 usage.
2. The declared unique-violation case surfaces SQLSTATE 23505.
3. Parameterized insert/select/upsert behavior matches the manifested subset exactly.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/psycopg2_params.rs and supporting fixtures.
Reservation guidance: keep this canary focused on psycopg2 parameterized behavior rather than broad ORM behavior.

title: Implement deterministic snapshot restore and clean reset semantics
description:
Context:
Restore/reset behavior is a separate operator contract from snapshot encoding and should be tested independently.

Deliverables:
- deterministic restore from committed-state snapshots
- clean reset semantics over the shared base/overlay model
- focused tests for restore idempotence and reset behavior
acceptance_criteria:
1. Restore from identical snapshots yields identical committed state.
2. Reset semantics are deterministic and bounded by the declared operator contract.
3. Restore/reset behavior is covered independently of budget measurement.
notes:
Target files: src/snapshot/restore.rs and restore-reset tests.
Reservation guidance: own restore/reset semantics only.

title: Map normalized kernel results and refusals into pgwire frames
description:
Context:
Simple-query, extended-query, and refusal paths all need one shared mapping layer from normalized kernel results into protocol frames. Without that layer, the adapter will duplicate frame logic and drift.

Deliverables:
- one mapping surface for AckResult, ReadResult, MutationResult, and RefusalResult
- shared frame encoding helpers for successful and refused outcomes
- focused tests for rows-affected, rowset, and refusal rendering
acceptance_criteria:
1. Protocol result mapping is shared instead of duplicated across simple and extended query paths.
2. Refusal results are rendered through the same normalized contract as successful results.
3. Result mapping does not reach into backend state directly.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/result.rs and result-mapping tests.
Reservation guidance: own frame mapping only.

title: Implement point lookups, filtered scans, predicates, and aggregate count/basic group-by
description:
Context:
The explicit phase-3 read lane needs separate scan, predicate, and aggregate slices so multiple agents can work without colliding and so kernel work does not wait on protocol shells. This issue is the core read-execution roll-up.

Deliverables:
- point lookup and filtered scan execution
- predicate evaluation and NULL semantics
- declared aggregate and basic group-by support only where manifested
acceptance_criteria:
1. Core scan, predicate, and aggregate child slices are all implemented through explicit beads.
2. The read executor depends on storage and read normalization, not on the protocol shell.
3. Unsupported read breadth remains explicitly out of scope.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/read.rs, src/kernel/predicate.rs, and src/kernel/aggregate.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Implement declared ON CONFLICT semantics over keyed storage
description:
Context:
ON CONFLICT is the highest-risk write behavior in the declared subset and should land only after insert, storage, and constraint surfaces are stable.

Deliverables:
- declared ON CONFLICT semantics for primary-key and unique-key cases
- focused parity cases for conflict detection and conflict resolution
- explicit refusal for unsupported conflict shapes
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared upsert cases match the shared differential write corpus.
2. Unsupported conflict shapes are refused explicitly rather than guessed.
3. Upsert behavior reuses keyed storage and constraint logic instead of duplicating it.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/upsert.rs and upsert-focused mutation tests.
Reservation guidance: own ON CONFLICT semantics only.

title: Build the declared differential read corpus against real Postgres
description:
Context:
The read parity lane now has shared differential runner/comparator support. This issue owns the declared read corpus itself plus suite wiring for those cases, not the shared harness machinery.

Deliverables:
- checked-in read/query fixtures and expected outcomes for the manifest subset
- suite wiring that feeds the shared differential support layer
- explicit SKIP/refusal classification for out-of-subset queries
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo contains a runnable read corpus using the shared differential support layer.
2. Declared read cases compare rowsets, ordering where claimed, and SQLSTATEs where relevant.
3. Unsupported query shapes are classified explicitly as refusal or SKIP.
notes:
Target files: tests/differential/read_corpus.rs and read fixtures.
Reservation guidance: own the read corpus and suite wiring only; shared diff support lives elsewhere.

title: Implement update/delete by predicate for the declared subset
description:
Context:
Update/delete semantics are a separate DML slice from catalog/session metadata stubs and should not be coupled. This issue now owns only update/delete by predicate for the declared subset.

Deliverables:
- update/delete by predicate for the declared subset
- focused SQLSTATE and rows-affected tests for declared update/delete cases
- refusal behavior for broader DML shapes
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared update/delete cases pass without widening the SQL surface ad hoc.
2. Rows-affected and SQLSTATE behavior are locked by focused tests.
3. Catalog/session metadata concerns stay out of this issue.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/update_delete.rs and update-delete tests.
Reservation guidance: own update/delete semantics only.

title: Implement overlay visibility, commit, and rollback semantics over the backend contract
description:
Context:
Overlay visibility and commit/rollback behavior are separate from the backend contract and should be isolated so memory-economics and correctness failures stay legible.

Deliverables:
- per-session mutable overlay visibility model
- commit/rollback semantics over overlay state
- focused visibility tests for reader/writer interactions
acceptance_criteria:
1. Readers see last committed state while one writer holds the mutable overlay.
2. Uncommitted writes remain session-local until commit.
3. Visibility semantics are covered by focused tests independent of snapshot encoding.
notes:
Target files: src/backend/overlay.rs and overlay-visibility tests.
Reservation guidance: own overlay visibility only.

title: Implement canonical full-content snapshot encoding and storage budget checks
description:
Context:
Snapshot and storage-economics work needs separate encoding, restore/reset, and budget-test slices so correctness and performance do not collapse into one broad patch. This issue is that roll-up.

Deliverables:
- canonical snapshot encoding and hashing
- deterministic restore/reset semantics
- storage-economics suite for startup/reset/private-RSS budgets
acceptance_criteria:
1. Snapshot encoding, restore/reset, and budget-test child slices are all implemented through explicit beads.
2. Determinism and budget regressions are caught by dedicated tests.
3. This issue remains a roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: src/snapshot.rs and tests/storage/*.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Normalize declared mutation shapes into MutationOp with refusal coverage
description:
Context:
The write path needs one focused normalization slice for the exact mutation subset claimed in the manifest.

Deliverables:
- normalization for INSERT, INSERT RETURNING, and declared ON CONFLICT shapes
- normalized table/column/returning surfaces for the mutation subset
- explicit refusal coverage for tempting but unsupported write forms
acceptance_criteria:
1. Supported write shapes normalize into stable MutationOp values.
2. Near-miss unsupported write forms become RefusalOp values before kernel execution.
3. Normalized mutation output is consistent across equivalent client-library shapes.
notes:
Target files: future normalization layer and mutation-focused tests.
Reservation guidance: keep this slice bounded to the declared write subset.

title: Implement pgwire startup, auth, parameter status, and session loop
description:
Context:
The protocol lane needs a credible startup/session shell before query work begins. This issue is the roll-up for startup handshake, startup metadata, and the connection session loop.

Deliverables:
- startup/auth handshake for declared clients
- backend key data and parameter status for the canary subset
- connection session loop and transaction-status state transitions
acceptance_criteria:
1. Startup/auth, startup metadata, and connection-loop slices are all implemented through child beads.
2. Declared clients can finish the startup shell without app-side hacks.
3. This issue remains a roll-up; query execution belongs to sibling beads.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/startup.rs, src/protocol/postgres/startup_metadata.rs, and src/protocol/postgres/session.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads, not direct claims.

title: Freeze the representative extractor fixture and input corpus for the live canary
description:
Context:
The extractor canary is risky because it spans fixture choice, input data, and live harness behavior. The representative extractor and its deterministic fixture set should be frozen first.

Deliverables:
- one pinned extractor entrypoint or script path for the canary
- deterministic fixture/input corpus for that extractor path
- clear in-repo documentation of the subset the canary is proving
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo contains one pinned representative extractor canary target.
2. The extractor fixture/input corpus is deterministic and checked in or otherwise reproducibly pinned.
3. The canary scope is documented tightly enough that future changes do not widen it by accident.
notes:
Target files: tests/fixtures/canaries/extractor_canary/** and extractor-canary fixture notes.
Reservation guidance: own fixture/corpus selection only.

title: Protect the Phase-0 bootstrap contract while v0 runtime work lands
description:
Context:
The current repo already powers working Phase-0 bootstrap pipelines. Runtime work must not silently break the existing CLI, report, snapshot, or refusal behavior while chasing shorter overall runtime. This issue is the safety roll-up for that constraint.

Deliverables:
- locked regression coverage for the current bootstrap CLI/report/snapshot contract
- explicit refusal and exit-domain coverage for current failure paths
- baseline startup/restore/reset budget capture so performance work has a reference point
acceptance_criteria:
1. Existing Phase-0 bootstrap behavior is covered by checked-in regression tests before runtime surfaces widen.
2. Current exit-domain and refusal behavior is locked by tests rather than memory.
3. A reusable budget harness exists so later runtime work can prove it did not regress the operator path.
notes:
Target files: tests/bootstrap_*.rs and tests/storage/*.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads, not direct code claims.

title: Implement canonical committed-state snapshot encoding and hashing
description:
Context:
Snapshot meaning is a separate contract from restore mechanics and budget measurement, so canonical encoding and hashing should land first.

Deliverables:
- canonical full-content snapshot encoding over committed state
- deterministic snapshot hashing over the declared v0 hash surface
- focused tests for excluded non-semantic fields
acceptance_criteria:
1. Identical committed state yields identical snapshot hashes across repeated runs.
2. Snapshot encoding uses full committed contents rather than overlay deltas.
3. Hash-surface exclusions are locked by focused tests.
notes:
Target files: src/snapshot/encode.rs and snapshot-hash tests.
Reservation guidance: own snapshot encoding and hashing only.

title: Implement input coercion and SQLSTATE 22P02 mapping for kernel values
description:
Context:
Type coercion is a separate failure surface from storage and constraint enforcement and should be isolated so parity failures stay legible.

Deliverables:
- coercion from client inputs into the declared runtime value model
- precise SQLSTATE 22P02 mapping for bad coercions
- focused coercion parity tests for declared types
acceptance_criteria:
1. Bad coercions surface SQLSTATE 22P02 rather than ad hoc parse errors or panics.
2. Coercion logic is reusable by insert, upsert, and later update/delete paths.
3. Coercion failures are covered by focused tests separate from storage semantics.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/coerce.rs and coercion tests.
Reservation guidance: own coercion logic only.

title: Implement live runtime lifecycle, --run orchestration, and final report/snapshot boundaries
description:
Context:
The plan makes startup, shutdown, --run, and final report/snapshot boundaries explicit, but the current Beads graph does not have a top-level lifecycle issue for them. This issue owns that missing runtime shell.

Deliverables:
- startup preflight ordering and bootstrap refusal boundaries
- orderly shutdown with one final committed-state report/snapshot boundary
- live --run child-process orchestration and final artifact emission
- exit-domain handling that preserves the 0 / 1 / 2 contract
acceptance_criteria:
1. Startup preflight order matches the plan before live traffic is accepted.
2. Interactive shutdown emits exactly one final report and snapshot over committed state when configured.
3. --run captures child exit/signal/timed_out metadata without inventing a second exit taxonomy for twinning.
4. Rolled-back mutations and transient session state stay outside report/snapshot semantics.
notes:
Target files: future src/runtime.rs, signal/lifecycle helpers, and run-mode tests.
Reservation guidance: own process lifecycle here; do not absorb protocol parsing, kernel semantics, or verify meaning into this lane.

title: Implement extractor_canary harness against the live twin
description:
Context:
The plan calls for one real extractor script to run unchanged against the twin. This issue is now the roll-up for extractor fixture freezing and the live harness wiring.

Deliverables:
- pinned representative extractor fixture/input corpus
- unchanged live extractor run against the twin
- focused subset assertions and failure output
acceptance_criteria:
1. Extractor fixture/corpus and live harness slices are both implemented through explicit beads.
2. The canary does not widen the claimed subset without manifest updates.
3. This issue remains a roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/extractor_canary.rs and tests/fixtures/canaries/extractor_canary/**.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Define normalized kernel result types for twinning
description:
Context:
The adapter also needs one normalized result contract back from the kernel before protocol work starts. This issue owns that result surface.

Deliverables:
- concrete Rust types for AckResult, ReadResult, MutationResult, and RefusalResult
- result tags and fields aligned to the plan
- result-shape boundaries that keep protocol mapping out of the kernel contract
acceptance_criteria:
1. The result module expresses exactly the normalized result family claimed in the plan.
2. Refusal results carry the fields needed for protocol-visible failures without encoding protocol frames.
3. Kernel callers can return normalized results without reaching into adapter-only types.
notes:
Target files: shared IR/result surface near future src/ir.rs.
Reservation guidance: own result contracts only; do not absorb operation normalization or protocol mapping.

title: Build in-memory row store and constraint executor for twinning
description:
Context:
The write kernel is a roll-up lane for value/coercion and storage/constraint/DML work. It should no longer directly depend on the full IR umbrella now that those semantic prerequisites are explicit on the leaf beads.

Deliverables:
- value/coercion lane
- storage/constraint lane
- declared write-subset parity through focused child slices
acceptance_criteria:
1. Differential write cases pass through focused child beads rather than one broad kernel patch.
2. Constraint and coercion failures surface the expected SQLSTATEs.
3. This issue remains the write-lane roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/** and mutation-focused differential tests.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Build Postgres compatibility and differential test harness for twinning
description:
Context:
The twinning SQL/session surface must be defined by a real canary corpus, not a prose support table. This issue is the roll-up for compatibility harnesses, differential corpora, and the checked-in manifest-backed subset contract.

Deliverables:
- client compatibility harnesses for psql_smoke, psycopg2_params, sqlalchemy_core, and extractor_canary
- differential harnesses that compare declared write/read cases against real Postgres
- consumption of the checked-in canary manifest as the normative source of truth for supported session and SQL shapes
- explicit SKIP/refusal classification for shapes outside the manifest
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo contains runnable compatibility harnesses for psql_smoke, psycopg2_params, sqlalchemy_core, and extractor_canary.
2. The checked-in canary manifest remains the normative source of truth for the v0 SQL/session subset.
3. The manifest, harness names, and fixtures line up one-to-one.
4. Differential cases compare observable behavior against real Postgres for the declared subset.
5. Unsupported shapes are classified explicitly as protocol-visible refusal or reporting-time SKIP.
6. This issue remains a roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/**, tests/differential/**, and canaries/manifest.v0.json.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via harness/corpus child beads.

title: Implement committed-state freeze and exactly-one final report/snapshot emission
description:
Context:
Final artifact meaning should be isolated from signal handling so committed-state freeze and exactly-once emission stay explicit.

Deliverables:
- committed-state freeze semantics for final artifacts
- exactly-one final report/snapshot emission when configured
- focused tests for omission of rolled-back and transient session state
acceptance_criteria:
1. Final artifacts are emitted exactly once over committed state only.
2. Rolled-back mutations and transient session state stay outside final artifacts.
3. Final artifact semantics are tested through direct finalization entry points independent of shutdown hooks and child-process orchestration.
notes:
Target files: src/runtime/finalize.rs plus artifact-boundary tests covering report and snapshot emission.
Reservation guidance: own final artifact boundaries only.

title: Bind committed twin relations into embedded verify inputs
description:
Context:
The verify bridge is still too broad for one sitting. Relation binding from committed twin state is a separate seam from report attachment and should land independently.

Deliverables:
- binding from committed twin relations into embedded verify inputs
- deterministic relation naming aligned with the compiled verify artifact
- focused tests for binding over committed state only
acceptance_criteria:
1. Embedded verify runs over named relations bound directly from committed twin state.
2. Binding excludes rolled-back or session-local state.
3. Relation binding is covered by focused tests independent of report formatting.
notes:
Target files: src/runtime/verify_binding.rs and verify-binding tests.
Reservation guidance: own committed-state relation binding only; report attachment stays in a sibling bead.

title: Implement --run child launch, wait, and termination orchestration
description:
Context:
Child-process orchestration is distinct from final report metadata and should be isolated so run-mode bugs are easier to diagnose.

Deliverables:
- child launch/wait/termination handling for --run mode
- focused tests for normal exit, signal exit, and timeout handling
- stable separation between child orchestration and artifact rendering
acceptance_criteria:
1. --run can launch, wait for, and terminate a child through one bounded orchestration path.
2. Timeout and signal behavior are covered by focused tests.
3. Child orchestration remains separate from final report formatting.
notes:
Target files: src/runtime/run_child.rs and run-child tests.
Reservation guidance: own child orchestration only.

title: Add shared rowset, tag, and SQLSTATE comparators for differential suites
description:
Context:
Parity suites need one consistent comparison path for observable outcomes, especially SQLSTATEs and explicit refusal-vs-SKIP handling.

Deliverables:
- comparators for rowsets, tags, and rows-affected outputs
- SQLSTATE and refusal/SKIP classification helpers
- stable failure formatting for parity regressions
acceptance_criteria:
1. Read and write parity suites share one comparison layer.
2. SQLSTATE parity checks are not duplicated across corpora.
3. Unsupported-shape outcomes are classified consistently as refusal or SKIP.
notes:
Target files: tests/differential/support/compare.rs.
Reservation guidance: own comparison logic only.

title: Emit backend key data and parameter status for declared client sessions
description:
Context:
Common clients rely on a small set of startup metadata before they will behave normally. This slice isolates that protocol metadata surface.

Deliverables:
- backend key data emission where the canaries require it
- declared parameter status frames for the supported client subset
- tests that lock the exact startup metadata surface used by the canaries
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared clients observe the expected startup metadata without ad hoc hacks.
2. The metadata surface is limited to what the canaries prove.
3. Startup metadata drift is caught by focused protocol tests.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/startup_metadata.rs and startup metadata tests.
Reservation guidance: own startup metadata only.

title: Build shared differential harness utilities for twin-vs-Postgres parity tests
description:
Context:
The current graph asks the write and read corpora to each invent their own comparison machinery. That is redundant and will make parity failures harder to interpret. This issue owns the shared differential harness layer.

Deliverables:
- one shared runner for twin-vs-Postgres corpus execution
- one shared comparison/result model for rowsets, tags, and SQLSTATEs
- one shared classification path for explicit refusal vs SKIP outcomes
acceptance_criteria:
1. Write and read parity suites reuse one shared differential support layer.
2. SQLSTATE and rowset comparison logic is not duplicated across corpora.
3. Differential failures are reported in a consistent, operator-readable format.
notes:
Target files: tests/differential/support/**.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child utilities, not direct claims.

title: Implement backend trait and per-session overlay visibility model
description:
Context:
Tournament-mode storage needs separate backend-contract and overlay-visibility slices so multiple agents can work without colliding. This issue is the backend/visibility roll-up.

Deliverables:
- backend trait and base snapshot access contract
- overlay visibility with commit/rollback semantics
- focused backend/visibility tests
acceptance_criteria:
1. Backend-contract and overlay-visibility child slices are both implemented through explicit beads.
2. Visibility semantics are proven independently of snapshot encoding and full mutation semantics.
3. This issue remains a roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: src/backend/base.rs and src/backend/overlay.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Implement table storage, key indexes, and constraint-backed insert/upsert semantics
description:
Context:
Once the value/coercion lane exists, the row-store needs a separate storage/constraint/DML sub-lane for the declared write subset. This issue is that roll-up.

Deliverables:
- table storage and keyed lookup surfaces
- constraint enforcement for the declared mutation subset
- INSERT/RETURNING and ON CONFLICT behavior through child slices
acceptance_criteria:
1. Storage, constraint, insert, and upsert child slices are all implemented through explicit beads.
2. Differential write parity is proven by the child slices, not by one monolithic patch.
3. This issue remains a roll-up; read breadth stays elsewhere.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/write.rs and src/kernel/constraints.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Implement value model and Postgres type coercion for row-store mutations
description:
Context:
The row-store needs one explicit value/coercion sub-lane before storage and DML semantics expand. This issue is the roll-up for the runtime value model and input coercion.

Deliverables:
- runtime value representation for declared column types
- input coercion with SQLSTATE 22P02 mapping
- focused value/coercion tests
acceptance_criteria:
1. Value-model and coercion child slices are both implemented.
2. Bad coercions surface SQLSTATE 22P02 through the dedicated coercion slice.
3. This issue remains a roll-up for value/coercion work only.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/value.rs and src/kernel/coerce.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Implement NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, and FK enforcement for mutations
description:
Context:
Constraint enforcement is the main correctness risk in the write lane and should be separated from row storage and DML orchestration.

Deliverables:
- NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, and FK enforcement for declared mutations
- focused SQLSTATE mapping for each declared constraint family
- constraint-only tests that fail before DML orchestration logic is involved
acceptance_criteria:
1. Constraint failures surface the expected SQLSTATEs without corrupting committed state.
2. Constraint logic is exercised by focused tests instead of only end-to-end DML cases.
3. The constraint layer remains separate from protocol framing and read execution.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/constraints.rs and constraint-focused tests.
Reservation guidance: own constraint enforcement only.

title: Implement point lookup and filtered scan execution over committed rows
description:
Context:
The read lane needs one core execution slice for point lookups and filtered scans, independent of protocol and aggregate behavior.

Deliverables:
- point lookup execution over committed row state
- filtered scan execution over declared read shapes
- focused rowset tests for deterministic core scan behavior
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared point-lookup and filtered-scan cases pass over committed state.
2. Core scan behavior is exercised independently of aggregate and catalog-stub behavior.
3. The read executor depends on committed storage and read normalization, not on the full write-kernel roll-up.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/read.rs and core read tests.
Reservation guidance: own scan execution only.

title: Add shared refusal and SQLSTATE assertions for compatibility canaries
description:
Context:
The compatibility suite needs one reusable assertion layer for protocol-visible refusals and SQLSTATE parity so each canary stays focused on client behavior.

Deliverables:
- shared helpers for expected SQLSTATE assertions
- refusal/assertion helpers for unsupported-shape checks
- common failure formatting for compatibility cases
acceptance_criteria:
1. Canary tests can assert SQLSTATE and refusal behavior through shared helpers.
2. Unsupported-shape checks use one common assertion path instead of ad hoc string matching.
3. Helper failures stay readable enough to debug compatibility regressions quickly.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/support/** assertion helpers.
Reservation guidance: own assertion ergonomics only; client-specific flows stay in the canary issues.
close_reason: Added shared canary SQLSTATE/refusal assertion helpers with focused tests

title: Implement minimal extended-query flow for parameterized canaries
description:
Context:
The extended-query lane is still too broad for one sitting if Parse/Bind state and Execute/Sync dispatch stay bundled. This issue is now the roll-up for those two execution seams only.

Deliverables:
- Parse/Bind state and refusal handling for the declared prepared-statement subset
- Describe/Execute/Sync dispatch over prepared state for the declared portal subset
- explicit protocol-visible refusal handling for unsupported extended-query shapes
acceptance_criteria:
1. Parse/Bind and Describe/Execute/Sync child slices are both implemented through explicit beads.
2. Parameterized canaries complete the declared extended-query path without widening the subset.
3. This issue remains a roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/extended_parse.rs and src/protocol/postgres/extended_execute.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Implement simple-query path and ACK mapping over normalized results
description:
Context:
With the startup/session shell in place, the adapter needs a bounded simple-query dispatch path for the declared smoke and session-control shapes. This issue no longer owns generic frame mapping.

Deliverables:
- simple-query dispatch for declared session-control and smoke SQL shapes
- use of the shared normalized-result frame mapping layer
- explicit refusal behavior for unsupported simple-query traffic
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared simple-query flows complete without protocol-local shortcuts.
2. Unsupported simple-query shapes return protocol-visible refusals rather than killing the session.
3. Frame rendering is delegated to the shared result-mapping slice.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/simple_query.rs and simple-query tests.
Reservation guidance: own simple-query dispatch only.

title: Define twinning operation IR and protocol-to-kernel normalization
description:
Context:
The twinning plan now makes the Operation IR explicit. We need one normalized contract between pgwire parsing and kernel execution before protocol and kernel tasks diverge in incompatible shapes.

Deliverables:
- define the v0 Operation IR types for SessionOp, PrepareOp, MutationOp, ReadOp, and RefusalOp
- encode normalization invariants for catalog resolution and unsupported-shape refusal
- add focused fixtures/tests that prove equivalent supported SQL shapes normalize identically
- keep protocol frames and backend residency hints out of the IR surface
acceptance_criteria:
1. Core IR/result, normalization, and equivalence-fixture child slices are all implemented through explicit beads.
2. Supported equivalent SQL shapes normalize to the same IR form and unsupported shapes become explicit refusal ops before backend execution.
3. This issue remains a roll-up only.
notes:
Target files: src/ir.rs plus normalization fixtures/tests.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Implement psql_smoke canary against live pgwire startup flow
description:
Context:
psql_smoke is the first compatibility claim in the manifest and the cheapest proof that startup/auth/session flow is credible.

Deliverables:
- runnable psql smoke harness against the live twin
- assertions for connect, parameter status, simple query, and rollback flow
- explicit refusal assertions for unsupported shapes exercised by the smoke path
acceptance_criteria:
1. psql can connect to the twin and complete the declared smoke flow without client-side hacks.
2. The harness fails on protocol regressions in startup/auth/session framing.
3. Unsupported live behavior seen in the smoke path is surfaced as a protocol-visible failure, not a process exit.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/psql_smoke.rs plus psql smoke fixtures.
Reservation guidance: this issue proves the psql canary only; broader write/query semantics stay elsewhere.

title: Implement predicate evaluation and NULL semantics for the declared read/write subset
description:
Context:
Predicate logic and three-valued NULL semantics are correctness-critical and should not be buried inside scan or update/delete orchestration.

Deliverables:
- predicate evaluation for eq, neq, lt, lte, gt, gte, is_null, in_list, and between
- three-valued NULL semantics for the declared subset
- focused predicate tests reusable by reads and update/delete
acceptance_criteria:
1. Declared predicate operators are covered by focused tests.
2. NULL semantics are locked independently of scan and DML orchestration.
3. Predicate logic is reusable by read and update/delete slices.
notes:
Target files: src/kernel/predicate.rs and predicate tests.
Reservation guidance: own predicate evaluation only.

title: Add golden bootstrap CLI and report regression coverage
description:
Context:
The current CLI/report path already works and must stay stable while runtime code is added around it.

Deliverables:
- golden-path tests for schema bootstrap and report emission
- assertions for unchanged top-level report fields and next-step messaging
- fixture-backed checks that current operator examples keep working
acceptance_criteria:
1. The current schema bootstrap CLI path is covered by a stable regression test.
2. Report contract drift causes a focused test failure instead of a silent behavior change.
3. The test stays within the existing implemented Phase-0 surface.
notes:
Target files: tests/bootstrap_cli.rs and bootstrap report fixtures.
Reservation guidance: own the bootstrap CLI/report regression only.

title: Implement live verify artifact intake and batch-only rule refusal
description:
Context:
The bootstrap path already loads verify-artifact metadata, but the live runtime still needs the real intake path and the rule-tier refusal boundary.

Deliverables:
- live runtime loading of compiled verify.constraint.v1 artifacts
- explicit refusal for any batch-only rule before live traffic is accepted
- report/reporting metadata that preserves artifact identity without reinterpreting semantics
acceptance_criteria:
1. Live twinning refuses batch-only rules before accepting traffic.
2. Verify artifact identity and hashes remain visible in the twin report.
3. Twinning still does not define or parse its own second constraint language.
notes:
Target files: src/runtime/startup.rs and verify-bridge startup tests.
Reservation guidance: own artifact intake and refusal semantics only; this belongs on startup preflight, not on protocol parsing.

title: Implement protocol-visible refusal mapping for unsupported live shapes
description:
Context:
Unsupported-shape error rendering is a separate concern from writer admission and should be isolated so contention policy can evolve independently.

Deliverables:
- default unsupported-shape SQLSTATE handling for live protocol refusals
- connection-safe error framing for protocol-visible failures
- focused tests for default 0A000 behavior and manifested narrower overrides
acceptance_criteria:
1. Unsupported live shapes default to protocol-visible SQLSTATE 0A000 unless a manifested narrower behavior exists.
2. Error framing keeps sessions alive where the plan requires it.
3. This issue does not own writer admission semantics.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/refusal.rs and refusal-mapping tests.
Reservation guidance: own unsupported-shape refusal mapping only.

title: Add shared live-twin launch and teardown utilities for canary tests
description:
Context:
Every compatibility canary needs the same process lifecycle plumbing to boot a twin, wait for readiness, and tear it down cleanly. This issue isolates that shared harness layer.

Deliverables:
- shared helpers to launch the twin under test
- readiness and teardown handling for canary runs
- common tempdir and port allocation behavior for compatibility tests
acceptance_criteria:
1. Canary tests can start and stop a twin through one shared helper surface.
2. Readiness and teardown failures produce actionable test failures rather than leaked processes.
3. New canaries can reuse this layer without reimplementing process plumbing.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/support/** once introduced.
Reservation guidance: own process harnessing only; do not encode client-specific assertions here.

title: Implement sqlalchemy_core canary against the declared read subset
description:
Context:
sqlalchemy_core is the first ORM-level compatibility claim for the declared v0 subset. It proves the twin can support a real transactional client without broad reflection claims, while keeping any required metadata stubs explicit and narrow.

Deliverables:
- runnable SQLAlchemy Core canary for connect/begin/commit/execute/fetch
- query fixtures tied to the declared manifest subset
- refusal assertions for shapes outside the subset when the canary touches them
acceptance_criteria:
1. SQLAlchemy Core can complete the declared phase-1/phase-3 flow without app-side hacks.
2. Row fetches and declared read/query shapes behave as claimed by the manifest.
3. Any metadata surface exercised by the canary is limited to bd-1ot; no broader reflection claim is implied.
notes:
Target files: tests/canaries/sqlalchemy_core.rs and supporting fixtures.
Reservation guidance: keep ORM compatibility narrow and manifest-backed.

title: Add IR equivalence fixtures for client-library shape normalization
description:
Context:
The plan requires proof that equivalent supported SQL shapes from different clients normalize identically. This issue owns those fixtures and tests.

Deliverables:
- fixtures that cover equivalent shapes emitted by psql, psycopg2, and SQLAlchemy Core
- assertions that the normalization layer produces stable equivalent IR
- refusal fixtures for unsupported but tempting near-miss shapes
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo contains focused tests proving equivalent client-library shapes normalize to the same IR.
2. Near-miss unsupported shapes are covered by refusal tests rather than left implicit.
3. The fixtures line up with the manifest-controlled subset.
notes:
Target files: normalization fixtures/tests under the IR lane.
Reservation guidance: keep this lane fixture-focused and do not re-implement parser logic inside tests.

title: Define Operation IR structs and controlled vocab for twinning
description:
Context:
The first missing implementation seam is the actual IR type surface. This issue owns the concrete structs/enums for the v0 operation family and nothing else.

Deliverables:
- concrete Rust types for SessionOp, PrepareOp, MutationOp, ReadOp, and RefusalOp
- controlled vocab for session ops, mutation kinds, read shapes, predicate operators, and aggregate kinds
- module boundaries that keep protocol frames and backend hints out of the IR
acceptance_criteria:
1. One shared Rust module exposes the full operation family named in the plan.
2. Controlled vocab values are encoded centrally rather than repeated ad hoc in callers.
3. The IR surface contains no pgwire frame structs and no storage-backend residency details.
notes:
Target files: future src/ir.rs or equivalent shared module.
Reservation guidance: own the operation type surface only; result types and fixtures stay in sibling issues.

title: Implement single-writer admission and contention reporting for pgwire sessions
description:
Context:
Single-writer admission is part of the v0 session contract and should not be buried inside generic error handling. This slice isolates writer gating.

Deliverables:
- single-writer admission checks for live sessions
- contention reporting with SQLSTATE 55P03
- tests that keep both sessions alive during writer contention
acceptance_criteria:
1. Conflicting writers receive SQLSTATE 55P03 while both sessions remain alive.
2. Writer admission policy stays distinct from generic protocol refusal mapping.
3. This slice does not widen the concurrency model beyond single-writer semantics.
notes:
Target files: src/protocol/postgres/writer_gate.rs and contention tests.
Reservation guidance: own writer admission only.

title: Implement orderly shutdown, committed-state freeze, and final report/snapshot emission
description:
Context:
The lifecycle lane needs explicit preflight, shutdown, and final-artifact slices so process control does not blur with report/snapshot meaning. This issue is the interactive-lifecycle roll-up.

Deliverables:
- startup preflight ordering and refusal boundaries
- interactive shutdown hooks
- committed-state freeze and exactly-one final artifact emission
acceptance_criteria:
1. Preflight, shutdown, and final-artifact child slices are all implemented through explicit beads.
2. Bootstrap/refusal boundaries stay process-level and explicit.
3. This issue remains a roll-up for interactive lifecycle only.
notes:
Target files: src/runtime.rs, src/report.rs, and src/snapshot.rs.
Reservation guidance: roll-up only; close via child beads.

title: Attach raw verify.report.v1 payloads and twin-native metrics to twin reports
description:
Context:
Report attachment is distinct from embedded verify execution. It should be isolated so final report-shape changes do not blur with relation binding or verify execution.

Deliverables:
- attachment of raw verify.report.v1 payloads to the twin report surface
- twin-native structural metrics emitted separately from verify outcomes
- focused report-shape tests for verify-present and verify-absent cases
acceptance_criteria:
1. Twin reports attach raw verify.report.v1 payloads without reinterpretation.
2. Twin-native structural metrics remain a separate report surface rather than being collapsed into verify.
3. Report-shape tests cover both verify-present and verify-absent outputs.
notes:
Target files: src/report/verify_attachment.rs and verify-report attachment tests.
Reservation guidance: own report attachment and twin-native metric surfacing only; verify execution stays in a sibling bead.

title: Integrate embedded verify execution and attach raw verify reports
description:
Context:
The twinning boundary is now explicit. twinning does not define a second constraint language, does not shell out to batch verify in live mode, and does not collapse constraint results into its own pseudo-score. It executes verify through the embedded surface and reports twin-native metrics alongside attached verify outputs.

Deliverables:
- consume compiled verify.constraint.v1 artifacts through the twinning runtime surface
- execute verify through the embedded verify library path over materialized twin state
- attach verify results in a report shape that keeps verify semantics intact
- report twin-native structural metrics separately from verify outcomes
- refuse batch-only verify rules explicitly in live twinning mode
acceptance_criteria:
1. Twinning does not define or parse a second constraint language.
2. Live twinning executes verify through the embedded library surface, not export-and-run batch invocation.
3. Twinning reports raw twin-native metrics and attached verify outputs without inventing a merged pseudo-score.
4. Batch-only verify rules are explicitly refused in live twinning mode.
5. The twin report carries the explicit v0 report contract, including run metadata when --run is used and omission of verify sections when no verify artifact is provided.
6. Benchmark scoring and assess policy remain outside twinning.
notes:
Target files: runtime/validation integration plus report surfaces that attach verify results without redefining verify semantics.
Reservation guidance: keep verify execution and attachment logic isolated from benchmark/assess concerns and align the report shape with the explicit v0 contract, including child-run metadata.

title: Implement interactive shutdown hooks and orderly termination handling
description:
Context:
Signal and shutdown handling is a separate concern from final artifact semantics and should be isolated so process-lifecycle bugs do not mix with report/snapshot meaning.

Deliverables:
- interactive shutdown hooks for live mode
- orderly termination handling for signal-driven shutdown
- focused tests for shutdown-path behavior
acceptance_criteria:
1. Interactive shutdown follows one orderly termination path.
2. Shutdown-path bugs are caught by focused lifecycle tests.
3. This slice does not own final artifact freeze semantics.
notes:
Target files: src/runtime/shutdown.rs and shutdown-signal tests.
Reservation guidance: own signal/shutdown handling only.

title: Add storage-economics suite for startup, reset, and private-RSS budgets
description:
Context:
Storage economics are part of correctness in this project, not just polish. The repo needs a dedicated suite for those budgets.

Deliverables:
- storage tests for startup, reset, overlay growth, and private-RSS budgets
- budget assertions aligned to the plan
- stable metric output for operator-visible performance regressions
acceptance_criteria:
1. The repo contains a dedicated storage-economics suite under tests/storage.
2. Startup/reset/private-RSS budgets are asserted through checked-in tests or benchmark gates.
3. Budget regressions are reported consistently enough to guide operator decisions.
notes:
Target files: tests/storage/*.rs.
Reservation guidance: own storage budget tests only.

title: Normalize declared read subset into ReadOp with refusal coverage
description:
Context:
The read lane needs one focused normalization slice for the canary-defined SELECT subset before execution work starts.

Deliverables:
- normalization for point lookups, filtered scans, predicates, and bounded aggregates in the manifest
- projection/predicate/grouping normalization for the declared subset
- explicit refusal coverage for unsupported query shapes
acceptance_criteria:
1. Supported read/query shapes normalize into stable ReadOp values.
2. Unsupported query forms become explicit refusal values before execution.
3. The normalization does not widen query breadth beyond the manifest-backed subset.
notes:
Target files: future normalization layer and read-focused tests.
Reservation guidance: keep this slice manifest-backed; do not invent generic SQL expression trees.
